item.title: Updated Image
item.content:encoded: Came back and updated this old thing to be in compliance with CC BY-SA, as the old image was still removed. It still warms my heart to see that this one managed to last.

item.title: New Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0, 2.5, 2.0, 1.0

Title: Sidewalks USA Kansas DouglasCo Lawrence Bellecrest Dr 3.JPG
Author: James Michael DuPont
Release year: 2014

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. Michigan, Ohio and airports.
I think we have a reality bending child who lives in the michigan/ohio area and has flown a bit and makes imagination games become real.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image appears to be from here, with no indication it has been released under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to cultural differences I really only get the one about the cracks and the floor is lava…

Can you explain the others to me? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey, this old thing.

I wrote this like 5 years ago and haven't done much with the site since, much to my chagrin. +80? Well that's a damn nice thing to come back to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guh, it said the last one didn't post, could someone delete this one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…which resulted in seventeen mild burns and one case of [DATA REDACTED]

Exsessive exspungement all through it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…which resulted in seventeen mild burns and one case of [DATA REDACTED]

Exsessive exspungement all through it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another favorite! 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is basically SCP-1000-J, but less than half as much fun and without a -J. Hell, the "step on a crack" superstition is played with in both articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i dont like it but the tone is decent enough so no vote, however i think that perhaps the "this one is easy enough to contain but hopscotch forever tho!" should be put above the -c incident collapse and not under unless your intention was to make it seem like the o5 was referring to the floor is lava as hopscotch?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted at 'the floor is lava!' because what if it really was, and I love the idea, but I strongly dislike both the researcher notes / requests (basically, the bits in italics), and the redaction in -E, because i couldn't tell what it was supposed to be. Reading the comments, it seems like some kind of American thing using a corruption of the classic nursery rhyme 'Ring a Ring o' Roses'. Also, bears eating you is what happens when you step on a crack (see: Hogfather, page 11).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ring Around the Rosie is not in fact about the black death. Of course, many people think it is, so the way I see it, it's still a valid background for that instance of the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ring around the rosie is about the black death. So it must release the bubonic plague. I've never heard of what B is based on though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The floor is lava! Stand on the couch! Oh god Jimmy's burning up!"
I think this idea is hilarious, but executed in a way that means it's still serious. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a somewhat common game of "using the furniture to cross the room" because the floor is "lava"or "deep water".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice idea. I can't count how many times we'd play "don't step on the red tiles" when I was in school, and turning that into an SCP is hilariously grim.

But seeing as there are five of these instances that do their own thing, I can't help but wonder what would happen if two or more of them were allowed to come into contact by spreading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to echo this sentiment. It could work with some sort of rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is -E ring-around-the-rosie? If so, it doesn't seem to fit very well with the rest of the games. A thru D are all of the "don't step here" type, while rosie doesn't pay attention to the ground at all.

Also, why redact the rhyme? This seems very dangerous, just begging for an accidental activation.

I think this could do with some editing on the staff speculation. An upgrade request sort of makes sense, it keeps popping up in the wild and they don't know why, but there is really no basis for speculations of sentience, targeting, or escalation.

Hopscotch forever is that rare creature, good lolfoundation. It isn't out of character, and it doesn't feel like you wrote the whole thing as a lead up to some godawful pun.

Upvote, for a good job on an idea most people would have said was impossible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All instances of SCP-1768 appear to follow their own set of rules,

I think that should be "appear to impose their own set of rules", since as-is it makes it sound like the SCPs themselves are playing games, rather than forcing humans to play games.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like idea of children's games manifesting, but I think I might like it better if the anomalous item were more general instead of listing five rather different examples. My thought is an area that manifests the various effects depending on which children are in the area. Then instead of having types A-E, these become examples of the way one area reacts to different sets of kids. I think this might streamline and focus it a bit. That would make 1768 something akin to the nursery in Bradbury's "The Veldt."

No vote from me for now, but, like others who have commented, I'm pleasantly surprised with how well this idea was brought into SCP form. As it stands, the nostalgia is just about right and it's currently safely in the positive double digits, so maybe I shouldn't be suggesting such a radical change as I have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is old-school. Not in a bad way, necessarily—I just think this SCP feels like it was written in 2009.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be better as a -J, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's pretty clever! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. Expected I was going to hate it, but it's just so damn charming. And while I don't want it to become a habit, the "Hopscotch for the rest of our lives" bit is genuinely hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the basic idea (children's games applying themselves to real life) but dislike the execution, particularly the staff commentary (as Lumancer pointed out), the expungments in the -E addendum, and several of the turns of phrase, particularly in the early description ("game" really doesn't seem like the right word here). Downvote here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [DATA REDACTED] is used less frequently but is allowed. I hate the use of it in this instance, but the phrase itself is kosher enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hnnngh… I don't like that it's being called a "game", and neglected to tag it as such. "Don't touch the carpet, it's made of lava!" doesn't really count as a game in my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The staff commentary is way too melodramatic for my tastes, and the rest doesn't really catch me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hesitant upvote from me, you've done a fairly good skip of something I wouldn't really consider skippable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think some of your measurements might be off; a carpet section of 20 by 15 meters is bloody enormous, and that's supposed to be just a section of a carpet- note that 20 meters is about 65 feet- just how big was this family's living room anyway? The other measurements aren't much better either, you should probably review them and take them down to a more appropriate size.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A nice tongue in cheek one. Once again, a skip explains a folk-tale, which I like
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[DATA REDACTED]

Not standard; should probably either be "REDACTED" or "DATA EXPUNGED".

Considering requesting upgrade to Keter if trend continues.

This seems unnecessary and kind of silly.

Also… did you seriously make "Step on a crack, break your mothers back", the floor is lava, and "Ring around the Rosie" into an SCP? I don't know whether I love you or hate you.

Ah, what the hell. Tentative upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, it reminds me of how I spent two weeks only walking on the blue tiles at my highschool; it started as a way of annoying another student, who believe stepping on a blue tile caused a random person to die, and then just became fun. My first reaction would be that this should be a -J, but it is handled in a rather good main list style

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure about this… I want to dislike it, but somehow it's keeping my attention. One small quibble -

"All instances of SCP-1768 appear to follow their own set of rules, which appear to be consistent with various informal games "

The double "appear" sounds a little clumsy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is kind of fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. Just one question. What is 1768-E referring to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think this was executed well. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As soon as I saw the picture I suspected this was a scip of the old saying, and I hated the idea. Then I saw that I was right, and I prepared to downvote with force.

Then I read it, and gosh darnit if I didn't think it was pretty good. +1
